atlas.experiment: e043 field universe (one GS field; division+inheritance+mutation+selection+adaptation+differentiation co-occur)
atlas.put_in: one Gray-Scott field of self-replicating spots; per-tissue continuous trait inherited+mutated at the growth front; fitness->kill coupled to a spatially-varying morphogen optimum tau*(x). Uniform tau=0.5 start. NO spatial domains / NO 'cells match optimum' put in
emerged: cells adapt to the local optimum: corr(trait, optimum) 0.942
ordered spatial trait domains: left 0.19 -> mid 0.483 -> right 0.841 (contrast 0.651)
surprises: from a UNIFORM sheet of identical low-trait cells, six field-ified life-processes co-occur in ONE field and self-organize a differentiated, locally-adapted body plan
atlas.persistence: the adapted trait is held per-tissue and re-established at each growth front under turnover
atlas.not_scripted_check: the occupied fraction, the correlation with the imposed optimum and the ordered domains are measured; selection could have failed to reach them
atlas.claim_tier: measured (occupied; corr with optimum; left->right domains) ; interpretive (the field-ified life-processes integrate into one self-organizing body) ; analogy (body/development)
floors: HYBRID: pure GS dynamics but the trait is a tracked per-TISSUE LABEL; the morphogen optimum tau*(x) is imposed (the environment), the differentiated body is emergent
'body / development / evolution' is analogy for the trait field
同じ数学≠同じもの; integrates e038 (object tracking) + e039 (morphogen) -- no new mechanism, it shows they co-occur in one field. (A) faithful: field law + trait rule + gradient are hand input
